title: egui App: chat list, message view, compose bar
description:
Implement the eframe::App trait on the main App struct in main.rs (or a new ui.rs module).

What: Full egui rendering pass producing a 3-panel chat interface.

Algorithm:
1. App struct holds: app_state: AppState, tx: mpsc::SyncSender<AppCommand>, rx: mpsc::Receiver<AppEvent>, ctx: egui::Context
2. In update():
   a. Drain rx with try_recv() in a loop; call app_state.apply_event(ev) for each; call ctx.request_repaint() after any event.
   b. TopBottomPanel::top('status'): show connection status + username.
   c. SidePanel::left('chats').default_width(220.0).show():
      - heading 'Chats'
      - For each chat in app_state.chats (sorted by last_message_at desc):
        - Button with chat name/contact + unread badge if unread_count > 0
        - If clicked: tx.send(AppCommand::SelectChat(chat.id.to_string()))
        - Highlight selected chat
   d. TopBottomPanel::bottom('compose').exact_height(60.0).show():
      - TextEdit::multiline(&mut app_state.compose_text).hint_text('Message...')
      - If Enter key pressed (and not Shift+Enter) OR Send button clicked:
        - tx.send(AppCommand::SendMessage { chat_id, body: compose_text.drain(..) })
   e. CentralPanel::default().show() (must be last):
      - If no chat selected: label('Select a chat')
      - Else: ScrollArea::vertical().stick_to_bottom(true).show():
        - For each message in app_state.messages:
          - Sender name (bold), timestamp, body text
          - Show [edited] if edited_at is Some
          - Show [deleted] if deleted_at is Some
      - Show error toast if app_state.error is Some.

Defensive rules:
- Never call unwrap() in update() path.
- Sanitize chat names: strip ASCII control characters before display.
- Message body: display as plain text regardless of bodyType (no HTML rendering).
- try_recv() loop must have iteration cap (max 100 events per frame) to prevent stall.

Tests: (ui is hard to test headlessly; test AppState logic via app_state_test.rs instead)
- update_drains_events: create App with pre-populated rx, call update() once, verify app_state updated
Oracle: AppState field values after update compared to expected values from injected events.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check -p jmap-chat-egui exits 0; cargo clippy -p jmap-chat-egui exits 0; app_state tests pass
close_reason: egui App with 3-panel UI; cargo check + clippy clean
